notes: Strongest moment: the examiner instantly recognized the 'already specified precisely' + explicit stop-signal case at turn 1, declined to manufacture an elenchus, and offered clean meta options (record as-is vs. probe one named aspect) rather than pushing past 'I'm done.' The brief is scrupulously honest, marking assumptions/open_questions/constraints empty and explicitly disclosing that no falsification probe ran. Weakest moment: the verdict field is labeled 'sharpened' even though nothing was sharpened — the prose transparently admits this, but a 'sharpened' tag for an accepted-as-is record is slightly generous. Type diversity is necessarily thin since only a single restatement-check question was appropriate here.
